title: Saudi Arabian Lamb Stew
description: A hearty and flavorful stew featuring tender lamb, rich spices, and a hint of bitterness, perfect for a crowd. This dish is a staple of Saudi Arabian cuisine, with a dense and satisfying texture.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes. Remove the lamb from the pot and set aside.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the ground cumin, ground coriander, salt, and black pepper to the pot and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the crushed canned tomatoes, dried fenugreek leaves, and browned lamb back to the pot. Stir to combine.
Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1.5 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Deglaze the pot with a little water, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
